Title: Verify that the planned route for display is sourced from Google Maps directions for the current trip segment
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - An active trip with a driver currently en route is in progress - Google Maps API is configured and reachable - GPS location data is being received
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the live map view - 3- Select the current active trip - 4- Observe the planned route data source used for that trip segment
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads successfully - 2- Live map view is displayed - 3- Active trip is highlighted on the map - 4- Planned route is dynamically fetched from Google Maps directions and corresponds to the current trip segment only

Title: Verify that the planned route is dynamic and updates based on real-time Google Maps recommendations
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - An active trip is in progress - Google Maps API is returning live traffic-aware route recommendations - Network connection is stable
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the live map view for an active trip - 3- Simulate a traffic change or road closure that causes Google Maps to recommend a different route - 4- Observe whether the planned route data updates accordingly
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Live map view and active trip are displayed - 3- Google Maps route recommendation changes due to traffic or road conditions - 4- The planned route used by the system updates dynamically to reflect the new Google Maps recommendation without requiring a manual refresh

Title: Verify that the planned route is restricted to the current trip segment only and does not include future or past segments
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - A trip with multiple segments is in progress - The driver is currently on the second segment - Previous and future segments exist
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the live map view - 3- Select the active multi-segment trip - 4- Observe which segment the planned route is based on
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Live map view is displayed - 3- Active trip with multiple segments is shown - 4- Planned route is based solely on the current in-progress trip segment and excludes all completed and future segments

Title: Verify that the planned route visual path is NOT rendered on the map (MVP exclusion)
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - An active trip is in progress - Google Maps API is available - The driver is en route to the next stop
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the live map view - 3- Select the current active trip - 4- Inspect the map display for any rendered route polyline or path overlay
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Live map view is displayed - 3- Active trip is visible on map - 4- No planned route line or path overlay is rendered on the map canvas - the map shows only vehicle position and stop markers

Title: Verify that only the time delay to destination is displayed and not the planned route or deviation overlay
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - An active trip is in progress - Google Maps API returns route and ETA data - Driver is currently delayed relative to planned schedule
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the live map view or trip detail panel - 3- Select the active delayed trip - 4- Observe what information is displayed related to the planned route comparison
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Map view and trip detail panel are displayed - 3- Active trip is selected - 4- System displays time delay to destination (e.g. "+5 min") without rendering the planned route path or deviation overlay on the map

Title: Verify that the time delay to destination displayed is calculated using Google Maps ETA compared to the scheduled arrival time
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - An active trip is in progress - Google Maps API is returning ETA data - A scheduled arrival time exists for the destination stop
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the trip detail panel for an active trip - 3- Note the scheduled arrival time for the current destination - 4- Compare the displayed time delay against the Google Maps ETA vs the scheduled arrival time
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Trip detail panel is displayed - 3- Scheduled arrival time is visible - 4- Time delay displayed equals the difference between Google Maps ETA and the scheduled arrival time and is accurate

Title: Verify that the planned route basis click behavior is scoped to the current trip only
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - Multiple trips exist - One trip is currently active - Others are scheduled or completed
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the live map view with multiple trip markers - 3- Click on the current active trip marker - 4- Attempt to interact with a completed or future trip marker and observe planned route behavior
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Map shows multiple trip markers - 3- Clicking active trip marker shows time delay info based on current trip Google Maps data - 4- Clicking completed or future trip markers does not trigger planned route calculation or display

Title: Verify system gracefully handles Google Maps API unavailability when retrieving planned route
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - An active trip is in progress - Google Maps API is temporarily unavailable or returning errors - Network connectivity is stable
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Simulate Google Maps API failure (e.g. invalid API key or API quota exceeded) - 3- Navigate to the live map view for an active trip - 4- Observe the system behavior and any error messaging
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Google Maps API failure is simulated - 3- Map view loads - 4- System displays a graceful error or fallback state (e.g. "Route data unavailable") without crashing - no broken UI elements or unhandled exceptions appear

Title: Verify system handles loss of internet connectivity while fetching planned route from Google Maps
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - An active trip is in progress - Internet connection is active initially and then disconnected mid-session
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal with active trip visible - 2- Navigate to the live map view - 3- Disconnect the internet connection - 4- Observe the planned route and time delay display behavior
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads with trip visible - 2- Live map view is displayed - 3- Internet is disconnected - 4- System shows a connectivity warning or stale data indicator - planned route data is either last known or shows unavailable - no crash occurs

Title: Verify that the time delay display updates automatically when Google Maps ETA changes due to traffic conditions
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - An active trip is in progress - Live traffic data is flowing and affecting Google Maps ETA - Time delay panel is visible
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the trip detail panel for an active trip - 3- Note the current time delay value - 4- Wait for a traffic event to change the Google Maps ETA - 5- Observe whether the time delay display updates
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Trip detail panel is displayed - 3- Initial time delay is noted - 4- Traffic event changes Google Maps ETA - 5- Time delay display refreshes automatically to reflect the updated ETA without requiring manual page reload

Title: Verify that planned route deviation overlay is absent from map when Planned Route vs Actual Deviation feature is excluded from MVP
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - Application is running an MVP build - An active trip is in progress - Driver has deviated from the originally planned path
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal (MVP build) - 2- Navigate to the live map view - 3- Select an active trip where the driver has deviated from the original path - 4- Inspect the map for any deviation highlight or planned route overlay
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Live map view is displayed - 3- Active trip with deviation is selected - 4- No deviation overlay or planned route comparison is rendered on the map - only time delay information is shown as per MVP scope

Title: Verify that no planned route polyline appears when driver is on track and no delay exists
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - An active trip is in progress - Driver is following the expected route - No delay is detected - Google Maps API is available
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the live map view - 3- Select an active on-time trip - 4- Observe the map display
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Live map view is displayed - 3- Active on-time trip is visible - 4- No route polyline or overlay is drawn on the map - vehicle marker and stop markers are visible - time delay shows zero or on-time status

Title: Verify time delay is displayed correctly in minutes when ETA difference is less than one hour
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - Active trip is in progress - Google Maps ETA exceeds scheduled arrival by 25 minutes - Time delay panel is visible
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the trip detail panel - 3- Observe the time delay format for a delay of 25 minutes
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Trip detail panel is displayed - 3- Time delay is shown as "+25 min" or equivalent minute-based format - no decimal hours or raw seconds are displayed

Title: Verify time delay is displayed correctly when ETA difference exceeds one hour
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - Active trip is in progress - Google Maps ETA exceeds scheduled arrival by 75 minutes - Time delay panel is visible
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the trip detail panel - 3- Observe the time delay format for a delay of 75 minutes
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Trip detail panel is displayed - 3- Time delay is shown in a human-readable format (e.g. "+1 hr 15 min") consistent with application formatting standards

Title: Verify time delay shows negative value or early indicator when driver is ahead of schedule
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - Active trip is in progress - Google Maps ETA is earlier than the scheduled arrival time - Time delay panel is visible
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the trip detail panel for a trip where driver is running early - 3- Observe the time delay display
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Trip detail panel is displayed - 3- Time delay shows a negative value (e.g. "-10 min") or an "Early" indicator reflecting that the driver will arrive before the scheduled time

Title: Verify that the planned route data refreshes when a new trip segment begins
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - A multi-segment trip is in progress - Driver completes first segment and begins second segment - Google Maps API is available
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the live map view for a multi-segment trip - 3- Observe the planned route basis as driver completes the first segment - 4- Observe whether the planned route updates automatically when the second segment begins
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Live map view is displayed - 3- Planned route is based on the first segment - 4- When second segment begins the planned route data refreshes automatically from Google Maps to reflect the new segment route and ETA

Title: Verify that the map UI layout correctly presents time delay information without a route overlay across different screen sizes
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - Active trip is in progress - Application is accessed on devices with varying screen sizes (desktop - tablet - mobile) - Google Maps API is available
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal on a desktop browser - 2- Navigate to the live map view for an active trip - 3- Note the time delay panel position and content - 4- Repeat steps 1-3 on tablet and mobile screen sizes - 5- Verify no route overlay appears on any screen size
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Desktop view shows time delay clearly without route overlay - 2- Tablet view shows time delay clearly without route overlay - 3- Mobile view shows time delay clearly without route overlay - 4- No planned route polyline appears on any device - layout adapts responsively

Title: Verify that the planned route basis works correctly when Google Maps returns multiple alternative routes
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - Active trip is in progress - Google Maps API returns multiple route alternatives for the current segment - Default route selection is in place
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the live map view for an active trip where Google Maps returns alternatives - 3- Observe which route is used as the planned route basis - 4- Verify only the recommended route is used
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Live map view is displayed - 3- Google Maps returns multiple routes - 4- System uses the first recommended (default) Google Maps route as the planned route basis - alternative routes are not displayed
